fingerprint.q: What is a browser fingerprint in this context?
fingerprint.a: It is a set of technical browser and device signals used by websites to identify sessions. BugLogin helps isolate and stabilize those signals per profile.

proxy.q: Can I run proxy and profile mapping consistently?
proxy.a: Yes. The workflow is built to keep identity and network configuration aligned so teams can reduce noisy account behavior.

automation.q: Is automation supported for repetitive tasks?
automation.a: Yes. Teams can combine API and automation routines to execute recurring actions while preserving workspace controls.

security.q: Can BugLogin help reduce multi-account suspension risk?
security.a: Yes. BugLogin focuses on profile isolation, stable runtime behavior, and controlled workspace operations so teams can run larger volumes more safely.

billing.q: How does billing fit into daily operations?
billing.a: Billing and entitlement stay visible on the web layer, while execution stays in desktop. Teams can upgrade, renew, and audit without disrupting runtime.

desktop.a: BugLogin fits teams handling multi-account marketing, e-commerce, social operations, or data workflows that need safer scale and tighter control.

team.q: How does team collaboration stay safe as we scale?
team.a: Workspace-level permissions, ownership boundaries, and action traceability help teams collaborate without losing control.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: The new BugLogin website is the front door for conversion, billing, and admin operations, so the terms focus on workspace access, governance, and acceptable use.

1.title: Usage scope
1.body: BugLogin accounts are provisioned for individuals or teams that have registered for the service. Every billing, auth, and admin action on the portal is tied back to a workspace and audit trail.

2.title: Governance and access
2.body: Platform admins, workspace owners, and workspace admins may only perform actions that are explicitly allowed by the active control plane permissions.

3.title: Payment compliance
3.body: Public purchases are processed through Stripe. Final payment state is determined by the billing authority and valid webhook processing.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: The new portal only collects and renders the data necessary for authentication, billing, and admin operations.

1.title: Account data
1.body: Email, user identifiers, avatars, and platform roles are used to authenticate users and route access correctly across the website.

2.title: Billing data
2.body: Workspaces, subscriptions, invoices, and usage metrics are synchronized from the billing and control plane systems into account billing and super admin.

3.title: Operational logs
3.body: Administrative actions may appear in audit logs to maintain traceability and operational safety for the platform.

refund.title: Refund policy
refund.description: Refunds are handled according to the active subscription state and the payment rules currently enforced by BugLogin.

1.title: Refund requests
1.body: Each request should be tied to a valid workspace, invoice, or checkout session so the team can verify the billing state accurately.

2.title: Processing window
2.body: The portal exposes invoice history and current plan state, while final refund handling follows the billing workflow defined by the system.

3.title: Impact on entitlement
3.body: After a refund or plan cancellation, the workspace entitlement may move according to the active, grace, or read-only policy.
